summary: TASK-20260428-004 did not use a real Generator Claude CLI run; it was implemented directly in the Codex/Analyst session and validated with dry-run/static checks.
details: Do not infer that scripts/run-generator.mjs hardening was itself produced by the Generator pipeline. Real Generator runs remain intentionally separate and require task/{TASK-ID} branch enforcement, JSON handoff, and post-run validation.

summary: Claude CLI --output-format json is not sufficient alone for 02-HARNESS Generator output correctness; pair it with --json-schema and wrapper-side normalization/validation.
details: Claude stdout may be a CLI JSON wrapper whose result field contains the model's structured answer. Store raw stdout separately and write a normalized generator-result.json only after required Generator fields pass validation.

summary: Post-task reusable operational insights need an explicit completion gate, not only session logs or reports.
details: Session logs preserve conversation and reports summarize a task, but future agents need compact reusable guidance. Use logs/insights.jsonl for discoveries that should survive session compaction and be considered during re-entry or handoff.

insight: git branch --merged <target> only works for fast-forward / non-squash merges. With squash merge, branch tips are never ancestors of target so --merged always returns empty for task branches. The correct check is comparing the branch tip tree to the FIRST commit on target whose message contains the task_id (the squash commit itself). Plain git diff target..branch is also misleading once target moves on to other tasks that touch the same files.

Cleanup-style automation should default to dry-run and require an explicit --force for destructive action. The current branch and target branch must be protected at the script level regardless of flag combinations. Each branch verdict should print its evidence (tree hashes, source commit) so users can audit decisions retroactively.

git-branch-policy.md instructs adding post-merge metadata (CURRENT_STATE, session log, MERGE_COMPLETED ledger event) to the squash commit BEFORE creating it. This makes the squash commit's tree strictly larger than the source task branch tip's tree, so the cleanup script's tree-equivalence check refuses to delete the source branch. Two robust fixes: (a) split into two commits on main like TASK-20260428-005 did (pure squash, then post-completion record), or (b) extend the cleanup algorithm to treat metadata-only differences in CURRENT_STATE/session/ledger as merged.

insight: Insight categorization (actionable_doc_change / gotcha / proposal / observation) plus an audit gate that blocks TASK_COMPLETED when actionable categories lack applied_to_doc=applied is the simplest enforcement that prevents 'forgotten learnings'. The gate is Tier-2 invariant: applies to all completion attempts equally, no per-task opt-in needed.

topic: CLI transport header metadata and non-ASCII workspace paths
insight: Codex/Claude/Gemini CLI session transports may include workspace path metadata in HTTP/WebSocket headers. Non-ASCII path components can trigger UTF-8 header conversion failures before the turn completes, so operational workspaces should use ASCII-only paths.

resolution_reason: Replace placeholder applied_to_doc.commit with the actual main commit for TASK-20260429-002.

topic: Claude Code --bare flag silently disables OAuth auto-load in headless mode
insight: Claude Code v2.1.x --bare flag's documented behavior covers hooks/plugins/MCP/auto-memory/CLAUDE.md auto-discovery skip. Empirically verified across 6 attempts on 2026-04-29: --bare also silently skips OAuth credential auto-load. Combination --bare --print fails with Not logged in even when CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var is explicitly set and propagated to the subprocess. Removing --bare while keeping --print + --output-format json + --no-session-persistence allows OAuth env var auth to work correctly. Trade-off: hooks/plugins/MCP/CLAUDE.md auto-load returns; context isolation must be enforced via --permission-mode + --allowedTools/--disallowedTools instead.

topic: Claude Code --bare flag silently disables OAuth auto-load (CLI options reference)
insight: Same root finding as INS-20260429-009-01 captured against the CLI options reference guide for cross-reference discoverability when operators look up --bare in the option table.

topic: Claude Code --json-schema does not constrain result field text content
insight: Claude Code --output-format json wraps response metadata (type, result, session_id, usage, etc.) as a JSON object. --json-schema flag is interpreted by Claude as a soft hint, not a hard constraint on the result field text content. Claude may return a natural-language report inside result while still completing the requested file changes correctly. Wrappers that JSON.parse(stdout.result) will fail on natural-language responses. Robust verification should rely on git diff for file changes, additional --output-format stream-json for tool-use events, or explicit prompt instructions to constrain output format.

topic: Generator wrapper output parsing assumes JSON; needs git-diff verification
insight: Same root finding as INS-20260429-009-02 captured against generator-pipeline-ko.md so operators reading the pipeline guide see this caveat. Concrete fix deferred to TASK-20260429-010 wrapper hardening: accept natural-language Generator reports + verify deliverable via git diff against task spec target_files.

topic: Generator wrapper target-file enforcement must use per-run baseline, not main...HEAD branch diff
insight: TASK-015 showed that checking the entire main...HEAD branch diff makes setup files look like Generator subprocess edits. The wrapper should capture pre-run dirty files and validate only files newly changed during the current Generator run, while still allowing known operational artifacts.

topic: Validator-A Windows codex.cmd shim requires cmd.exe mediation from Node
insight: TASK-015 showed that PowerShell-visible Codex CLI commands may not spawn from Node the same way. On Windows, direct spawnSync(codex.cmd, ...) can fail with EINVAL; run-validator-a should route .cmd/.bat shims through cmd.exe /d /s /c and record the actual spawn command in metadata.

topic: Do not close repeatable guide/process defects as report-only findings
insight: When a task discovers a guide error, stale guide text, wrapper behavior mismatch, or repeatable operational defect, the completion path must not rely on memory or the final report alone. The same task must update a guide, enforce a wrapper/audit script where feasible, or link an explicit follow-up task before completion.

topic: Codex approval policy flag belongs before exec in current CLI
insight: The installed Codex CLI accepts approval policy as a top-level option (`codex -a never exec ...`), not as an exec subcommand option (`codex exec ... -a never ...`). The latter exits with `unexpected argument '-a'` before any Validator result can be captured.

topic: Codex structured output schemas require strict object additionalProperties false
insight: The current Codex structured-output path rejects response schemas whose object schemas do not explicitly use `additionalProperties: false`. Validator result schemas used with `--output-schema` must be strict at the root and nested object levels, with nullable fields used for optional values.
